sourceMovie.title: The Bourne Supremacy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Bourne Supremacy for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Greengrass keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Identity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Bourne Supremacy excels in action, The Bourne Identity (2002) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is the natural next step after The Bourne Supremacy: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Bourne Supremacy who respond to its action will find Extraction 2 (2023) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Repo Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Bourne Supremacy fans keep landing on Repo Men (2010) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Those Who Wish Me Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Those Who Wish Me Dead draws from the same action well as The Bourne Supremacy, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Retribution
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Retribution (2023) captures the action appeal of The Bourne Supremacy without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Retribution at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Long Kiss Goodnight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of The Bourne Supremacy — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into The Long Kiss Goodnight (1996). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Beguiled
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Beguiled (1971) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps The Bourne Supremacy rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Nighthawks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nighthawks (1981) is the kind of film The Bourne Supremacy fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Alone in Berlin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Alone in Berlin shares The Bourne Supremacy's gift for drama — the 2016 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Bourne Supremacy?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Identity, The Bourne Ultimatum, Extraction 2—each shares DNA with The Bourne Supremacy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Bourne Supremacy?
faq.answer: The Bourne Supremacy (2004) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Bourne Supremacy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Bourne Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
